methods.description: Process for collecting gypsum and limestone samples from the outcrop at the Makhmur Gypsum Quarry site.

steps.description: Five samples of gypsum were collected from the outcrop.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect 5 gypsum samples from a specific geological outcrop.

steps.description: one sample of limestone were collected from the outcrop.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect 1 limestone sample from a specific geological outcrop.

methods.method_name: Gypsum Sample Preparation for Chemical Analysis
methods.description: Process for preparing collected gypsum samples by washing, drying, grinding, and sieving to obtain a fine powder suitable for chemical analysis.

steps.description: The samples of gypsum were washed
and ground by a tema mill.
steps.searchable_summary: Grind dried gypsum samples using a tema mill.

steps.description: The resulting powders were sieved with a 150μ sieve
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve ground gypsum powder using a 150μ sieve.

steps.description: and the portion that passed through the sieve was kept in closed boxes for use in chemical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Store the sieved gypsum powder (<150μm) in closed boxes.
outputs: Prepared gypsum powder stored for analysis

methods.description: Procedures for measuring the chemical composition (CaO, SO3, H2O+, I.R.) of prepared gypsum powder samples using various wet chemistry techniques. Analysis was performed on samples both before and after calcination.

steps.description: The (CaO %) content was measured volumetrically for each powder by titration of calcium oxide (CaO) solution of the unknown sample with 0.01M EDTA solution using murexide as an indicator.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure CaO content (%) using volumetric titration with EDTA and murexide indicator.

steps.description: The (SO 3 %) content was measured gravimetrically by barium chloride (BaCl 2 ) to precipitate sulphate ions (SO 4 2-)
steps.searchable_summary: Measure SO3 content (%) using gravimetric analysis by precipitating sulphate with barium chloride.

steps.description: The (H 2 O + %) content was measured by heating 1 gm of the powder sample in a muffle oven at 400 o C for 1 hour and calculating the weight loss
steps.searchable_summary: Measure H2O+ content (%) by heating a 1 gm sample at 400C for 1 hour and calculating weight loss.

steps.description: The insoluble residue (I.R.%) was measured by dissolving 5 gm of the powder sample in 10 ml of 10% HCl and weighing the residue.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure insoluble residue (I.R.%) by dissolving a 5 gm sample in 10% HCl and weighing the remaining residue.
